creator: dominik@wombacher.cc
text:
I don't know if that helps you to track down the issue, but it seems like that I run into the same problem without even using the Port. I never installed openzfs, I just use what's shipped with FreeBSD and by updating from 13.0-RELEASE-p13 to 13.1-RELEASE-p7, I wasn't able to access an encrypted dataset anymore, with similar errors messages as reported in other comments.

I was able to downgrade to 13.0-RELEASE-p13 as workaround to get access to my data again. I created the encrypted dataset Mon Dec 20 16:04 2021, I can't recall which FreeBSD version was running at this time, based on the date and given the fact that I updated 5 days before based on some logs I could found, it was probably either 12.3 or 13.0.


13.1-RELEASE-p7:

[root@web ~]# zfs mount -l -a
cannot mount 'zroot/bastille': Input/output error
cannot mount 'zroot/bastille/backups': Input/output error
cannot mount 'zroot/bastille/cache': Input/output error
